title: Online store accessibility in Ireland (EAA)
metaDescription: Since 28 June 2025, S.I. 636/2023 requires online stores in Ireland to meet WCAG 2.1 AA — with criminal liability for serious breaches. Free accessibility scan.
ogDescription: Your store must meet WCAG 2.1 AA since 28 June 2025. Ireland is the only EU state with criminal liability — up to €60,000 or 18 months. Keep dated evidence.
h1Html: Is your online store breaking Irish law?
answerHtml: Since 28 June 2025, S.I. 636/2023 requires online stores selling to consumers in Ireland to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Your checkout is the highest-risk page. Ireland is the only EU state with criminal liability — up to €60,000 or 18 months, with directors personally liable. Only true microenterprises are exempt.

keyFacts.title: The rules for stores at a glance

sections.h2: What must an accessible online store meet?
sections.bodyHtml: Your store must meet WCAG 2.1 AA — around 50 testable criteria: full keyboard operability with a visible focus, sufficient colour contrast, alt text, correctly labelled form fields, and a screen-reader-usable navigation. The checkout and payment flow are the most critical pages.

Ireland is the only EU state where serious non-compliance is a criminal offence — up to €60,000 or 18 months on indictment, with directors personally liable. A dated evidence trail is your defence. Accessibility fines →

sections.h2: Does it apply to small stores?
sections.bodyHtml: Only true microenterprises are exempt — under 10 staff and under €2M turnover. Both must be true, so most commercial stores are covered.

sections.h2: How do you make your store provably accessible?
sections.bodyHtml: Test your checkout against WCAG 2.1 AA, fix the violations, publish your accessibility statement, and keep a dated record proving conformity. keepcite audits, fixes and documents. Start with the free scan below.

scan.sub2: Your store, scanned against the law’s standard.
scan.leadHtml: Selling online in the EU? The European Accessibility Act applies to you.
scan.subject: New scan request — keepcite (IE · online shop)

items.a: No. A theme is a base, but your content, plugins, customisations and the checkout decide real conformity. What’s audited is the code you actually ship.
It’s the highest-risk page and the first thing an audit drives with a keyboard and screen reader. A store can look fine and still be impossible to buy from.

items.q: Do I need an accessibility statement?
items.a: Yes, a public accessibility statement is mandatory — conformance status, known limitations and a way to report barriers, matching reality.
The obligation has applied since 28 June 2025, and Ireland’s liability is criminal — a dated record that begins before any complaint is the strongest protection.

footer.noteHtml: This page passes the audit it sells: WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard-navigable, screen-reader tested. · © 2026 keepcite
